text: AI is increasingly common in the workplace. Automation handles repetitive tasks, like data entry and scheduling, which saves time and reduces errors. Productivity tools, such as smart email assistants and project managers, help workers focus on more creative or strategic work. Human-AI collaboration is becoming essential, with people using AI for analysis and decision-making support. However, these changes also mean some jobs will shift or even disappear, while new roles requiring AI skills are created. To adapt, workers need to learn how to work alongside AI, embracing it as a helpful partner rather than a threat. This balance can lead to more efficient and innovative workplaces.
